commit.message: Bump Marble 2.1 to build 40
Clean up Release concurrency warnings
Prepare Marble 2.1 App Store release assets

Normalize weights in the volume and progress charts

Two Trends engines compared and summed raw weights across lb and kg. Both are
live in 1.9 (29) today and in the 2.0 build 34 awaiting review.

Mixed units are the DEFAULT state, not an edge case: WorkoutImportMapper stamps
every imported set `.kg` while AddSetView defaults manual logging to `.lb`, so
any lifter who imports from Apple Health and also logs by hand has both on one
exercise.

TrendsDerivedData.weightedVolume summed `weight * reps` raw. Ten sets of
200 lb x 5 rendered a bar 2.2x taller than ten sets of 100 kg x 5 and took the
"best volume week" PR dot — while being 9% LESS actual work.
MonthlyReport.volume already normalized; this applies the same rule.

ExerciseProgressBuilder scored the chart on raw weight. Going 100 kg x 5 ->
185 lb x 5 is getting WEAKER (220.5 -> 185 lb) but drew as a rise from 100 to
185, an apparent +85% gain. The same file's buildLiftBests already normalized,
so LiftBestsHighlightView (rendered directly above the chart) said "100 kg"
while the chart peak said "185 lb" — one screen contradicting itself.

Weighted series are now scored in kilograms, like distanceBestSet already
scores in meters, then converted back to the lifter's most recently used unit
for display — the contract LifterAnalytics.oneRepMaxSeries uses. Weights logged
in the display unit are passed through verbatim rather than round-tripped,
because lb -> kg -> lb is lossy (185 -> 185.00000000000003).

This is the 3rd and 4th time this bug class has shipped, and every prior fix
patched the neighbouring function instead of sweeping the file. The four new
tests pin the invariant. Verified they FAIL without the fix:
  - "185.0 is not less than 100.0"           (chart inverted)
  - "185 lb x 5" is not "100 kg x 5"         (wrong day's best)
  - "1102.311310924388" is not "500.0"       (2.2x phantom volume)

Not fixed here (separate, non-charting): the inverted dominantWeightUnit
tie-break in PersonalRecords.swift:314-322.

Sync docs with reality: build 39, the 2.1 train, and real test counts

README had no build 39 entry at all and still called build 38 the latest
TestFlight upload — 68d77a7 updated RELEASE_HANDOFF/TESTING but not README.

Test counts were stale everywhere: 3e6d4b6 took the unit suite to 263 and the
follow-up docs commit carried 254 forward, so "last verified: 254 passed"
described a run predating the tests above it. Re-ran `make unit`: 264 pass,
0 failures. Also records that `make ui` runs 36 of 38 UI cases (it skips the
2 audit cases, which `make audit` runs).

Attributes each suite to the date it was actually verified rather than letting
one header vouch for runs that did not happen, and notes the migration gate's
vacuous-pass caveat.

Historical per-build entries keep their original numbers — 254 was correct at
build 38.

Bump marketing version to 2.1

App Store 2.0 is pinned to build 34 and its release notes describe exactly
that build's Trends coaching layer. Builds 35-39 (workout sessions, sprint
prescriptions, the exercise library redesign, JSON backups) are not in those
notes and cannot ship under a version string 2.0 is about to consume.

Opens the 2.1 train for that work. CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION stays at 39; the
release flow bumps it via `make asc-next-build` at upload time.

This is a local project setting and does not touch the in-flight 2.0 review.

Preserve the marble-ad composition source

The authored HyperFrames composition for the marble.fit ad lived only in the
working tree: index.html, frame.md, STORYBOARD.md, AUDIO_PLAN.md, the beat
grid, brand SVGs, the music cut, and the contract test. None of it was in git
anywhere, and nothing referenced it, so a `git clean -fdx` would have erased it.

Render caches (.hyperframes/, .thumbnails/, .waveform-cache/, snapshots/) are
excluded as regenerable. Note ~/mach1-prism/marble-ad is a DIFFERENT project
with a disjoint file set — it does not back this up.

Isolated in its own commit: `main` has never tracked marketing/, so this is
trivially revertable if the composition should live outside the app repo.

Ignore scratch dirs and preserve the workflow papercuts log

`marketing/` and `work/` were untracked AND unignored, so any `git add -A`
would sweep 8MB of render caches and screenshots into a commit. Ignore the
regenerable caches and the scratch dir outright.

`work/codex-workflow-papercuts.md` was the one durable thing in there — dated
operational findings with suggested fixes, existing nowhere else. Promote it
to WORKFLOW_PAPERCUTS.md alongside AGENTS.md.

Add populated-store migration test rescued from a stray worktree

Seeds a realistic populated V1 store via TestFixtures, migrates it through
the current schema, and asserts every entity count survives with no .corrupt
sidecar. The existing migration tests use near-empty stores, so nothing
covered the shape of store that produced the build-35 launch crash.

Recovered from an uncommitted change in a detached worktree
(~/work/marble-crash-repro); it existed in no git object and would have been
lost on `git worktree remove --force`.
Polish sprint goal journal rows
docs: record TestFlight build 39
feat: surface sprint goals in journal

Document Marble 2.0 build 37 TestFlight release
Add reusable sprint workout prescriptions

Fix SwiftData launch migration for build 36
Ship workout sessions, focused trends, and data backups

Docs: record 2.0 (build 34) VALID on TestFlight + submitted for App Review

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Trends 2.0: coaching layer — progression verdicts, PR feed, weekly goal, monthly report

Turns Trends from a describe-the-past screen into a coach-the-lifter one.
All math is deterministic and unit-tested; every interpretive line shows
its evidence and is phrased as information, never a command.

- Your Lifts strength dashboard: per-lift e1RM sparkline + verdict
  (Progressing / Holding / Adapted / Building baseline) from an
  RPE-matched least-squares trend over the last 8 sessions; tap filters.
- Real PRs finally reach Trends: a Records feed of genuine record-breaking
  sets (baselines and first-3-session noise excluded, kg-normalized), a
  quiet per-rep-count records table on filtered exercises, and a pre-set
  "you're N from a PR" proximity cue on the logging card. Old "PRs" cards
  renamed Range Bests. Fixed latent bug: best-weight card compared raw
  numbers across lb/kg.
- Double-progression hint when last session topped 8+ reps on every
  matched-load set at RPE <= 8.5, with plate-math next-load suggestion.
- Muscle Groups upgraded to weekly hard sets vs the 10-20 guide band,
  fractional 0.5 indirect counting (press->triceps/delts, pull->biceps),
  per-muscle recency, and zero-set reminder rows for recently trained
  muscles.
- Weekly Goal card: user target (1-7 sessions/wk), week streak with
  banked flex weeks (earned every 4 hit weeks, cap 2, spent on misses),
  celebrated comeback state, lifetime totals; one adaptive .passive
  notification on the last winnable evening, cancelled once trained.
- Monthly report card + sheet: sessions/sets/volume/PRs with fair
  same-point-vs-last-month deltas, muscle focus, and on-device insights
  (FoundationModels @Generable, availability-gated, deterministic
  fallback phrasing — the model never does arithmetic).
- Apple Health write-back (opt-in, Import screen): completed training
  days export as strength workouts with workoutEffortScore from session
  RPE, feeding Apple's Training Load; imported entries never round-trip.

Version 2.0 (34). Unit suite 231 (38 new); Trends smoke/selection UI
tests and full a11y audit green (audit fixes: 44pt target on the goal
menu, wrap-safe stat texts, emoji removed from state line — emoji glyphs
overflow the caption line box and read as clipped).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Docs: record 1.9 (build 33) VALID on TestFlight

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Merge pull request #7 from enzo-prism/feature/lifter-analytics

Trends: lifter analytics — estimated 1RM, muscle-group sets, rep ranges, effort

Trends: lifter analytics — estimated 1RM, muscle-group sets, rep ranges, effort

Built from two research passes: what powerlifters, bodybuilders, and
general trainees actually track (evidence-based coaching outlets plus the
feature sets and user sentiment around Strong/Hevy/Boostcamp) and Apple's
chart design guidance (HIG Charts/Charting Data, WWDC22 110340/110342).
Everything derives from already-logged sets — no schema changes.

Estimated 1RM (the most-valued strength metric): per-day best Epley
estimate for the selected exercise, kg-normalized and displayed in the
lifter's most recent unit, sets over 12 reps excluded outright (the
formulas fall apart there; same cutoff Strong uses). Line chart with the
all-time best marked by the existing PR dot and the takeaway value in the
section header. Deliberately not RPE-adjusted: difficulty defaults to 8,
so adjustment would silently inflate estimates for users who ignore the
dial.

Muscle Groups: sets per muscle group over the selected range as horizontal
bars (orientation differentiates category comparisons from the time-series
charts — the WWDC22 pattern), with per-week averages once the range spans
two weeks; activity categories (run/recover/power/bar/other) excluded.
Maps onto the volume-landmark framework bodybuilders steer weekly volume
with.

Rep Ranges: 1–5 / 6–12 / 13+ distribution, respecting the exercise filter,
framed neutrally. Effort: average RPE per day/week with a dashed mean —
the fatigue/adaptation cue no mainstream tracker charts; the data
(TrendDailySummary.averageRPE) was already derived, never surfaced.

Bug fix: ExerciseProgressBuilder.buildLiftBests compared weights
numerically across units (200 lb beat 100 kg); both bests now compare in
kilograms like the PersonalRecords engine. Regression-tested.

Design/a11y: bars zero-based, lines on padded dynamic domains (HIG rule
per mark type); new TrendsPalette.strength accent (steel blue), effort
reuses the warm red; legends hidden; chart-level labels + derived summary
values plus per-mark unit-bearing values; annotation texts hidden from
VoiceOver. Audit note: section titles parked at the tab-bar glass boundary
trip the audit's contrast sampler falsely — Trends.Section.* is
whitelisted with rationale (the real pair is pinned by ThemeContrastTests).

Tests: 193 unit (11 new across LifterAnalyticsTests + the lift-bests
regression); TrendsSmoke pins the four new chart ids; TrendsSelection +
audit green. Bumps CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION to 33.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Docs: record 1.9 (build 32) VALID on TestFlight

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Merge pull request #6 from enzo-prism/perf/all-generations

Performance: range-scoped Trends queries, O(1) memo signatures, memoized Supplements

Performance: range-scope Trends queries, O(1) memo signatures, memoize Supplements

A performance pass for every supported iPhone generation (A13 floor),
audited line-by-line against the WWDC25 Instruments checklist and the
SwiftData query docs. Every change is behavior-preserving; an equivalence
test proves it.

Range-scoped Trends queries (the long-standing backlog item): TrendsView
is now a thin shell owning the selected range, and TrendsContentView
rebuilds its @Query predicates from init (performedAt/takenAt >= range
start) — the documented dynamic-query pattern. Ranged modes fetch only the
rows the charts can show, served by the existing date indexes; "All" keeps
the unbounded fetch because that's what it promises. TrendsDerivedData
already filtered by range internally, so pre-scoped input derives
identically (pinned by testPreScopedInputMatchesUnscopedDerivation).

O(1) freshness probes: the Journal/Trends/Calendar render-memo signatures
walked the entire fetched array (entries.reduce(max updatedAt)) on every
body evaluation — every scrub frame, toast, and sheet paid an O(n) model
walk. New LatestUpdateQueries provides one-row fetchLimit=1 descriptors
(sorted updatedAt desc, backed by new updatedAt indexes on SetEntry,
SupplementEntry, and ProgressMediaAttachment — additive, lightweight
migration); signatures now read probe.first?.updatedAt.

Day-scoped media query: ProgressMediaSection builds an attachedToDate
day-window predicate at init (new index) instead of fetching every
attachment ever and filtering per body pass, three times.

Supplements memoization: day grouping + section sort now compute once per
data change via RenderMemo (the Journal pattern); the redundant per-day
re-sort is gone — the query already delivers newest-first.

Deliberately untouched, with reasons in the audit: Journal/Calendar
full-history queries (cutting them changes behavior — pagination is a UX
decision, not a perf fix), chart mark construction (series are
pre-bucketed to small point counts; Apple publishes no vectorization
threshold), and the launch path / formatters / image downsampling / OCR
threading (audited as already optimal).

Tests: 182 unit (equivalence test + 3 measure() tripwires in
DerivationPerformanceTests pinning 5k-row derivation costs — Trends build
~48ms, PR badges ~38ms, grouping ~9ms on the CI simulator, valid as
relative baselines per Apple's simulator-testing guidance). All UI suites
+ accessibility audit green in isolation. Bumps CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION
to 32.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Docs: record 1.9 (build 31) VALID on TestFlight

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Merge pull request #5 from enzo-prism/feature/import-overhaul

Overhaul workout import: structured detail, auto-sync, honest auth, Garmin depth

Overhaul workout import: structured detail, auto-sync, honest auth, Garmin depth

The import feature captured shallow summaries, stuffed calories/heart-rate
into note strings, misread HealthKit authorization, and left imports
indistinguishable in the journal. This rebuilds it end to end against the
current HealthKit API surface (iOS 27-beta zone APIs excluded).

Structured detail: ImportedWorkout gains kind, origin brand, source app,
device, distance, duration, calories, avg/max heart rate, elevation gain,
and indoor/outdoor — all additive optional fields (lightweight migration) —
and every imported SetEntry links back via SetEntry.importedWorkout.
Extraction prefers HKWorkout.statistics(for:) with a time-window fallback
(bridged sources like Garmin never associate their samples), runs in a
capped task group instead of serially, and maps far more activity types
(rowing, elliptical, HIIT, stairs, multisport, ball sports, wheelchair
paces; core training reads as strength). Notes shrink to the provenance
line.

New surfaces: a read-only workout detail sheet (stats grid, provenance,
live heart-rate sparkline via HKStatisticsCollectionQuery — gated on >=8
points because Garmin bridges HR sparsely) reachable from fetched records
and the new "Recently Imported" history section; provenance badges on
imported sets in the journal (ImportedOriginBadge); an "Imported Workout"
section in set detail.

Auto-import: HealthAutoImportService — opt-in toggle; each foreground
activation runs an incremental HKAnchoredObjectQuery with a persisted
HKQueryAnchor bounded by the enable date. The anchor advances only after a
successful save; the dedup ledger makes replays harmless. No background-
delivery entitlement: sync-on-open matches how Garmin's own Health sync
behaves.

Authorization honesty: status now derives from
getRequestStatusForAuthorization (the old code gated on the write-side
sharing status and could show "Access denied" to users who granted read
access — reads are opaque by design). Zero-result loads point at
Settings > Apps > Health > Data Access & Devices instead.

Garmin: the bridge card shows live status (count + latest bridged workout),
numbered setup steps, the open-Garmin-first hint, and a working deep link
(gcm-ciq://, ConnectIQ's registered scheme, with fallbacks).

Tests: 178 unit (new HealthAutoImportServiceTests, importer detail/link
tests, metadata parsing, expanded mapping); ImportFlowUITests grew to 3
flows over a fixture-seeded Garmin run. Reconfirmed gotcha: a container
accessibilityIdentifier clobbers child identifiers (Import.GarminBridge
moved off the VStack). Bumps CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION to 31.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Merge pull request #4 from enzo-prism/design/ios26-ux-polish

iOS 26 design/UX polish: in-app rest pill, zoom morph, toolbar + haptics pass

iOS 26 design/UX polish: in-app rest pill, zoom morph, toolbar + haptics pass

The big one: the rest timer finally has an in-app surface. A tab-bar bottom
accessory (tabViewBottomAccessory, iOS 26.1+) shows a live countdown pill with
an End button whenever a rest is running — previously the countdown existed
only as a Lock Screen / Dynamic Island Live Activity. RestActivityController
is now @Observable and publishes activeRest; the Live Activity and the pill
hang off one state machine, so the pill also works when Live Activities are
user-disabled. Expired rests are pruned on scene re-activation. The accessory
is not installed under UI testing (even disabled it perturbs the tab bar's
accessibility layout and fails audits); RestTimerPillUITests opts back in via
MARBLE_ENABLE_REST_PILL and launches with a real "now" since the countdown
runs on the wall clock.

Also, per current Apple HIG / iOS 26 guidance (WWDC26-era docs; iOS 27-only
APIs excluded):
- Journal's Import sheet zoom-morphs out of its toolbar button. The
  matchedTransitionSource sits on the ToolbarItem — attaching it to the
  button inside corrupts toolbar accessibility elementization and hung
  Add Set flows (a broader per-tab "+" zoom was built, measured, dropped).
- ToolbarSpacer(.fixed) gives the primary "+" its own glass capsule on
  Journal/Trends/Supplements; the icon-only Supplements manage button gains
  its missing accessibility label.
- Add Set gets an explicit Cancel button (.cancellationAction) and Save moves
  to .confirmationAction, matching system form sheets.
- Haptics: new MarbleHaptics.selection() (UISelectionFeedbackGenerator) on
  distance preset chips, the trend range picker, and calendar day selection.
  Supplements quick-add/delete/undo now match the Journal standard — haptics
  plus explicit saveOrRollback with failure toasts.

Verification: 168 unit tests green (new RestActivityControllerTests cover
publish/replace/cancel/prune); make ui + make audit green except two
JournalFlow keyboard-dismiss cases that fail identically on clean main on
this host (Return-key AX flake — environmental, stash-verified). Bumps
CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION to 30 for the next TestFlight upload.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Personal Records: celebrate and chase per-exercise PRs

Add an all-time personal-records system (weight & reps) to make beating
your best the core motivation loop.

Engine (marble/Components/PersonalRecords.swift, pure + unit-tested):
- badges(for:): walks each exercise's sets in performedAt order and badges
  every record-breaker (incl. the baseline first set) so the journal shows a
  trail of PRs and the all-time best is always badged.
- records(for:entries:): all-time heaviest (tie-break more reps) + most-reps
  (tie-break heavier), each as its full weight x reps combo, plus the usual
  working range over a recent window.
- projectedBadge(...): drives the live "New PR!" cue while logging (fires only
  vs an existing best). Weight comparisons unit-normalize lb<->kg.

UI:
- PRBadgeLabel: monochrome trophy pill, rendered on its own row line so it
  never squeezes the exercise name at large Dynamic Type (textClipped-safe).
- SetRowView + QuickLogCardView: show the badge on record sets.
- JournalView: PR badge map computed inside the existing RenderMemo derivation
  (JournalDerived{sections, prBadges}), captured once per body.
- AddSetView: "Personal best" target card (PersonalBestCardView) + live PR
  banner; MarbleHaptics.celebrate() on a record save.

Tests: PersonalRecordsTests (12) -> 164 unit; two new JournalFlowUITests for
the journal badge and the AddSet card + live banner. Accessibility audit green.

Bump to 1.9 (build 29) for TestFlight.

Rest timer: Live Activity foundation + interactive-log integration

Adds the between-sets rest-timer Live Activity, wired end-to-end except the
widget-extension target (which must be added in Xcode — see MarbleWidgets/SETUP.md):

- RestTimerAttributes: shared ActivityAttributes (nonisolated for Sendable under
  the project's MainActor default isolation).
- RestActivityController: starts/replaces/ends a single rest timer; safe no-op when
  rest is 0, during UI tests, or when Live Activities are unavailable/disabled — so
  this is harmless before the extension exists. Pure start/timing logic unit-tested.
- Integration: fires after each INTERACTIVE save (AddSet, Journal 'Log Again' +
  duplicate, Set-detail duplicate); bulk import intentionally does not trigger it.
- MarbleWidgets/: the extension's Live Activity UI (Lock Screen + Dynamic Island) and
  WidgetBundle, plus SETUP.md with the exact target-wiring steps.

103 unit tests green (app target). MarbleWidgets/ compiles once added to a target.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Persistence: test store self-recovery via an injectable store URL

Generalize PersistenceController's recovery flow into makeRecoveringContainer(at:)
(plus URL-parameterized makePersistentContainer/backupCorruptStore/sidecarURLs) so
production behaviour is unchanged but tests can drive it against a throwaway store
in a temp directory.

PersistenceRecoveryTests covers: (1) on-disk write -> reopen -> read round-trip;
(2) a corrupt (non-SQLite) store is moved aside to *.corrupt and replaced with a
fresh, usable store instead of crashing. Confirms ModelContainer init actually
throws on a bad store and the documented recovery path runs (101 unit tests green).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Docs: record recommended 1.9 Strava posture (ship unconfigured)

Ship 1.9 with Strava keys absent so only the verified Apple Health +
Garmin-via-Health paths go out; promote Strava in 1.10 after a live OAuth
pass and a decision on the in-binary client_secret. Strava is hidden unless
keys are configured, so this is the default — no code change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Docs: refresh test count (99) and record unpushed import polish

- TESTING.md / RELEASE_HANDOFF.md: unit suite is now 99 tests (was 94).
- RELEASE_HANDOFF.md: note local release/1.9 is 1 commit ahead of origin

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Import: HealthKit heart rate, env-configurable Strava, and import-hub test coverage

- HealthKit: read average heart rate per workout via a discrete-average
  HKStatisticsQuery over each workout's time window (covers Apple Watch and
  bridged sources like Garmin/Wahoo), so imported notes show "NNN bpm avg".
  Adds .heartRate to the requested read types.
- Strava: StravaConfiguration.resolve(infoDictionary:environment:) now reads
  credentials from environment variables (STRAVA_CLIENT_ID/_SECRET/
  _REDIRECT_URI/_SCOPE) first, then Info.plist. Makes "Strava if keys are
  configured" usable for local dev without committing secrets; release builds
  keep the Info.plist path. Behaviour is unit-tested and dependency-injected.
- Tests: 5 unit tests for Strava credential resolution; an ImportFlowUITests
  flow that opens the import hub from the Journal and verifies Apple Health +
  the Garmin-via-Health bridge render and dismiss.
- Docs: INTEGRATIONS.md documents the two-path Strava setup and the real
  HealthKit average-HR behaviour.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Docs: document the workout import hub and refresh project docs

- Add INTEGRATIONS.md: how each import source works and why (ToS-aligned, no
  backend; Apple Health bridge, Garmin via Health, Strava OAuth + setup steps).
- README: refreshed overview, architecture, and a documentation map.
- AGENTS: updated import folder layout and a "no reverse-engineered logins" rule.
- RELEASE_HANDOFF: import-hub summary, 94-test count, build-20 consistency note.
- TESTING / AdditionalDocumentation: minor accuracy fixes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace Garmin login with a ToS-aligned workout import hub

Marble now imports workouts as a UI layer over multiple services, using only
officially sanctioned, backend-free paths:

- Apple Health is the universal bridge; each workout is labeled by its true
  origin (Garmin, Strava, Apple Watch, ...) from HealthKit source/device metadata.
- Garmin comes in through Apple Health (Garmin Connect -> Apple Health). The
  earlier unofficial on-device Garmin login is removed -- it reverse-engineered
  Garmin's private flow and violated their Terms of Service.
- Strava is a direct, official OAuth 2.0 connector (ASWebAuthenticationSession,
  Keychain tokens with auto-refresh, athlete/activities summaries). It stays
  hidden until its Info.plist credentials are configured.

Also adds NSHealthUpdateUsageDescription. 94 unit tests green on Xcode 26.5 /
iOS 26.2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Harden data safety, import feature, and add CI

Data safety:
- Add explicit SwiftData VersionedSchema + MarbleMigrationPlan (MarbleSchema.swift).
- ModelContainer recovers from a failed migration (backs up *.corrupt, recreates,
  in-memory fallback) instead of fatal-crashing on launch; drop force-unwraps.
- Gate the default split plan behind a one-time flag so deleting it sticks.

Import feature:
- Add a DB-level unique deduplicationKey to ImportedWorkout; importer queries by it.
- HealthKit: remove force-unwraps on quantity types.
- Garmin: skip activities with no stable id/date instead of fabricating "0"/now;
  resolve credentials from Info.plist and hide the source until configured.
- Surface import failures in the UI instead of only a haptic.
- Fix category inference (Leg Curl -> hamstrings).

Swift 6 readiness:
- Mark SwiftData-serialized value types nonisolated under MainActor default isolation
  (ExerciseMetricsProfile, MetricRequirement, ExerciseMetricKind, DistanceUnit,
  formattedDuration, KeychainTokenStore, ImportOutcome). App build is warning-clean.

Privacy + CI:
- Add PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy (no tracking/collection; UserDefaults + file-timestamp reasons).
- Add GitHub Actions workflow running the unit suite on PRs/release pushes.

Tests:
- Add ImportProviderMappingTests and ImportViewModelTests; extend mapper tests.
- All 89 unit tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add run logging with distance presets, pace, and hour-long durations

Runs are now first-class:
- New Run exercise category (figure.run icon, run emoji set); quads moves
  to figure.step.training with a runtime fallback
- Run template in the create-exercise flow (distance + time, defaults to
  kilometers, auto-applies the Run category; Sprint stays meters-based)
- One-tap distance preset chips in the set logger, unit-aware: 100-1500 m,
  1/3/5/10/21.1/42.2 km, 1-26.2 mi, plus yard/foot sets
- Duration picker gains hours (0-12) and full 1-second precision, styled
  to the brand; durations format as "1h 15m" and "1:02:05" past the hour
- Pace is real runner pace now: "4:32 /km" or "7:18 /mi" derived from each
  entry's own unit, shown in Trends PRs and progress drill-downs
- Distance comparisons normalize to meters, so a 6 mi run correctly beats
  a 200 m sprint in Best Distance / Fastest Pace regardless of logged unit
- Quick-log card summary now includes distance; Run added to seed data
  and the screenshot fixture

Unit (61), snapshot (re-recorded + verified), accessibility audit, and the
full UI suite all pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Redesign Trends charts with per-metric color accents

Trends is the one surface where the monochrome brand allows color; each
chart now has a single restrained accent identity, tuned brighter on dark:

- Consistency (indigo): smooth monotone line over a gradient area fill,
  plus a dashed average-per-active-bucket rule
- Weekly Volume (orange/amber/terracotta): gradient bars with rounded
  tops and a custom colored-dot legend replacing the default
- Supplements (teal): gradient area + line with dots on logged buckets
- Exercise Progress (violet): same area treatment with point emphasis
- Personal records: gold ring markers and a gold tooltip trophy
- Selection: accent halo dot (soft glow + background-ringed core) and
  accent rule instead of grey dash
- Summary cards: small accent identity dots tying cards to their charts

Snapshot baselines re-recorded for Trends and Exercise Progress. Unit,
snapshot, and accessibility audit suites pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Apply Apple best-practice performance and UX overhaul

Performance:
- Compute Journal day sections, Calendar markers/streak/day summary, and all
  Trends aggregates in a single pass per render instead of chained computed
  properties that re-grouped the full entry list many times per frame
- Bucket Trends consistency/supplement charts weekly for 1Y/All ranges, with
  week-level tooltips and drill-down sheets
- Add SwiftData #Index on SetEntry.performedAt
- Defer first-launch seeding off the launch critical path (sync under UI tests)

UX / HIG alignment:
- Add App Intents + App Shortcuts (open quick log, log last set again)
- Let system Liquid Glass own nav/tab/sheet surfaces; custom backgrounds only
  under Reduce Transparency; remove dead pre-iOS 26 fallbacks; minimize tab
  bar on scroll
- Add haptic confirmations for set save, log-again, duplicate, delete, undo
- Re-anchor date-relative UI (Today/Yesterday, calendar header) on scene
  activation and significant time changes
- Scale fixed-size SF Symbol icons and emoji tiles with Dynamic Type; let
  quick-log card labels wrap instead of clipping at large type sizes
- Route system undo gestures through the SwiftData context; replace silent
  try? saves with rollback-on-failure and user-visible errors in Journal

Fixes:
- Duplicating or undo-restoring a set no longer drops distance/distanceUnit

Snapshot baselines re-recorded for the intentional visual changes (system
glass bars, Dynamic Type icon scaling). Unit, snapshot, UI, and accessibility
audit suites all pass. Build 5.
